sections.paragraph:
fut. יִטַּע, inf. נְטֹועַ and טַעַת [to set any thing upright, so that it is fixed in the ground; cognate roots are יָצַע, وضع to place, יָצַג Hiph. הִצִּיג id.; compare also נָצַב and יָצַב, and in the Indo-Germanic languages Sanscr. dhâ, Greek τίθημι. Thes.] to plant, prop. a tree, a garden, a vineyard, Gen 2:8 9:20 Lev 19:23 Num 24:6 followed by two acc. to set a garden with anything, Isa 5:2 also with acc. of the place filled with plants (bepflanzen), Eze 36:36. Figuratively it is said, to plant a people, i.e. to assign them a settled residence (compare the Germ. ein Bolk verpflanzen, die Pflanzftadt). Amo 9:15 Jer 24:6, “I will plant them, and will not pluck them up,” Jer 32:41 42:10 45:4 Psa 44:3 80:9 Exo 15:17 2Sa 7:10 compare the opp. נָתַשׁ, also מַטָּע and יָתֵד Ezr 9:8.

(2) to fix, to fasten in, as a nail, Ecc 12:11.

(3) to pitch a tent, Dan 11:45 hence the tent of heaven, Isa 51:16 to set up an image, Deu 16:21.

Niphal, to be planted, metaph. Isa 40:24.

Hence מַטָּע and the two nouns which follow.
